text.title: AI Feature Eval Set Design Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the ai-feature-eval-set-design methodology end-to-end.

step.name: harvest-canonical-cases
step.action: Collect ≥30 representative happy-path inputs with expected outputs. Each row labeled with the feature criterion it exercises.

step.name: harvest-adversarial
step.action: Convert ≥10 known-failure scenarios into adversarial rows. Each row pairs failing input with the expected correct output.

step.name: sample-drift-rows
step.action: Sample ≥5 rows representative of the production distribution. Label each manually for ground truth.
step.output: Drift rows table; sampling rule documented

step.name: choose-judge
step.action: Open-ended → LLM-as-judge with Pydantic rubric. Closed-form → rule-based matcher. Pin the judge model + version.
step.decision-gate: If task is open-ended AND ground truth is deterministic (e.g. classification), prefer rule-based — cheaper, deterministic.

step.name: wire-ci-gate
step.action: Add a CI job that runs eval on PR diff, compares to baseline, fails if any axis regresses > 2pp. Surface failing row IDs in the PR comment.
step.output: CI live; baseline stored in repo.
